title: How God Restores
executive_summary: > **A Note Before We Begin** > > This article contains no physics. No equations. No neuroscience. Just Scripture and the process it describes for how God brings a person from the bottom back to life. I'm writing it this way on purpose — because the Bible de... Because the connection was restored and the life flows. "Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!" (2 Corinthians 5:17). The packet separates publishable language from unresolved math, axiom, and contradiction obligations.
plain_english: > **A Note Before We Begin** > > This article contains no physics. No equations. No neuroscience. Just Scripture and the process it describes for how God brings a person from the bottom back to life. I'm writing it this way on purpose — because the Bible described this process completely, precise... Every restoration begins in the same place. Not in church. Not in a moment of inspiration. In the dirt. David — the king, the warrior, the man after God's own heart — wrote from there: "I waited patiently for the Lord; he turned to me and heard my cry. He lifted me out of the slimy pit, out of the mud and mire; he set my feet on a rock and gave me a firm place to stand" (Psalm 40:1-2). In simpler terms, the article is trying to establish: Because the connection was restored and the life flows. "Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!" (2 Corinthians 5:17).

contradictions.text: But the Bible doesn't need the science.
He would not even look up to heaven, but beat his breast and said, 'God, have mercy on me, a sinner'" (Luke 18:13).

Scripture answers with extraordinary consistency: start by giving thanks. "Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his love endures forever" (Psalm 107:1). "Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.
For a person coming off the floor of the pit, acknowledging that waking up is a gift — not a right, not an accident, but a gift from Someone — is a revolution.

It's acknowledging that God is Lord over the small things — not just the big spiritual decisions, but the Tuesday afternoon snack, the evening screen time, the words you're about to say to your spouse.
Elijah — exhausted, suicidal, hiding in a cave — heard God not in the dramatic display but in the whisper that came after. "My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me" (John 10:27).
Paul catalogs it: "But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control" (Galatians 5:22-23).

What You Do Scripture is precise about the division of labor: **What God does:** - Responds to surrender: "Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you" (James 4:8) - Provides the power: "For it is God who works in you to will and to act in order to fulfill his good purpose" (Philippians 2:13) - Sends the Spirit: "And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another advocate to help you and be with you foreve...
But the Bible described the path first, named the mechanism first, and promised the outcome first.

seven_q_items.text: Because in the pit, one thing happens that cannot happen anywhere else: you stop trusting yourself. --- ## The Surrender "I can't do this." Four words that change everything.
The surrender isn't the first step of a self-improvement program.
This is the practice that distinguishes surrender from passivity.
You don't have to decide in the moment whether to surrender.

What You Do Scripture is precise about the division of labor: **What God does:** - Responds to surrender: "Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you" (James 4:8) - Provides the power: "For it is God who works in you to will and to act in order to fulfill his good purpose" (Philippians 2:13) - Sends the Spirit: "And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another advocate to help you and be with you foreve...
Pit to surrender to thanksgiving to permission to listening to daily practice to transformation to testimony.

claims.text: Because the connection was restored and the life flows. "Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!" (2 Corinthians 5:17).
